extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity) requires that flipping severe_headache from false to true, with all other inputs fixed, never lowers acuity. severe_headache appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which forces acuity := self_care (the lowest value) when chest_pain ∧ palpitations ∧ slurred_speech ∧ severe_headache. Because priority 100 dominates all other rules, activating this rule overrides any escalation.

Counterexample: chest_pain=T, palpitations=T, slurred_speech=T, pallor=F, rash=F, abdominal_pain=F.

Baseline (severe_headache=FALSE): interaction_drop cannot fire (missing severe_headache). Matching rules are raise_chest_pain(p2→urgent), raise_slurred_speech(p2→urgent), raise_palpitations(p3→emergent). Highest priority is p3 → acuity = emergent (3).

After flip (severe_headache=TRUE): all four conjuncts of interaction_drop are now satisfied, so interaction_drop(p100→self_care) fires and, having the highest priority, wins. acuity = self_care (0).

Acuity drops from emergent(3) to self_care(0) purely by setting severe_headache true. This violates monotonicity. The witness assignment given is the after-flip state; the baseline differs only in severe_headache=false yielding emergent vs self_care after the flip.
